title: orobanche uniflora subsp. occidentalis
intro: Orobanche uniflora subsp. occidentalis, also known as Western Broomrape, is a parasitic flowering plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders, such as diarrhea, nausea, and bloating, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. Orobanche uniflora subsp. occidentalis is typically prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal purposes, with the dried roots and leaves being used in herbal remedies.
